item.title: Why Rising Gas Prices Could End the Republican Majority
item.author: Dan Pfeiffer from The Message Box
item.content:encoded: By raising gas prices for an unpopular war Trump has undermined his last remaining political asset. ͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­ View in browser<https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubWVzc2FnZWJveG5ld3MuY29tL3Avd2h5LXJpc2luZy1nYXMtcHJpY2VzLWNvdWxkLWVuZC10aGU_.LYdvj_T_YPqfyFuR_bqbHSJfJ6barg5o2rmkQ9zUfc8?><https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubWVzc2FnZWJveG5ld3MuY29tL3Avd2h5LXJpc2luZy1nYXMtcHJpY2VzLWNvdWxkLWVuZC10aGU_.GCHdd_QSGSYjDp6mZjTA2FkCaDyw2UxWcHvJADOV0WY?> Why Rising Gas Prices Could End the Republican Majority<https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=65026&post_id=191067357&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=false&r=183vj&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjoyMDU3ODg3LCJwb3N0X2lkIjoxOTEwNjczNTcsImlhdCI6MTc3Mzc0NDY3MSwiZXhwIjoxNzc2MzM2NjcxLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNjUwMjYiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.T4x7cmUT5bMXMRl7pw6w3oyxKlA9D_vLSCe_6_yfuM0> By raising gas prices for an unpopular war Trump has undermined his last remaining political asset. Dan Pfeiffer<https://substack.com/@messagebox> Mar 17 ∙ Paid<https://substack.com/@messagebox><https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=65026&post_id=191067357&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=false&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjoyMDU3ODg3LCJwb3N0X2lkIjoxOTEwNjczNTcsInJlYWN0aW9uIjoi4p2kIiwiaWF0IjoxNzczNzQ0NjcxLCJleHAiOjE3NzYzMzY2NzEsImlzcyI6InB1Yi02NTAyNiIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.dXNHLBkml7MPFMldSFvoBZnm54LrJL6MIszFT-Oe4gw&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=183vj><https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=65026&post_id=191067357&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=false&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjoyMDU3ODg3LCJwb3N0X2lkIjoxOTEwNjczNTcsImlhdCI6MTc3Mzc0NDY3MSwiZXhwIjoxNzc2MzM2NjcxLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNjUwMjYiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.T4x7cmUT5bMXMRl7pw6w3oyxKlA9D_vLSCe_6_yfuM0&r=183vj&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email><https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=65026&post_id=191067357&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=false&r=183vj&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjoyMDU3ODg3LCJwb3N0X2lkIjoxOTEwNjczNTcsImlhdCI6MTc3Mzc0NDY3MSwiZXhwIjoxNzc2MzM2NjcxLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNjUwMjYiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.T4x7cmUT5bMXMRl7pw6w3oyxKlA9D_vLSCe_6_yfuM0><https://substack..fzmni7nFecBdq58yjB4V04CPdo_KPCxBl5NWjWJX9ZQ?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email> <https://open.substack.com/pub/messagebox/p/why-rising-gas-prices-could-end-the?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app> READ IN APP In the Spring of 2012, Barack Obama gathered his political team for a meeting on the state of the reelection campaign. Arrayed around the table in the Roosevelt Room were the senior campaign staff, the pollster, and his top White House advisors. During the meeting, the President received updates on polling, fundraising, and the field organization in the battleground states. The media team previewed ads going on the air. All of the updates were positive and everyone, including the President, was feeling pretty upbeat. And then David Plouffe, the White House Senior Advisor and mastermind of the 2008 campaign, spoke up. “Gas prices are an existential threat to the entire enterprise. If they keep going up, we will lose reelection.” The direness of Plouffe’s warning caught everyone’s attention. Plouffe was known for keeping calm under the most intense pressure and never panicking. Gas prices, of course, came down and Obama won reelection — but Plouffe’s concern highlighted an important truth: high gas prices are a political killing field for incumbents. If high gas prices were bad for Obama in 2012, they are exponentially worse for Trump in 2026. Here’s why: Trump Gets the Blame Voters get pretty grumpy, pretty quickly, when gas prices go up. And they have noticed. According to the Ipsos Consumer Tracker<https://substack.com/redirect/112fb505-22e2-451c-9a5d-c3e634450f18?j=eyJ1IjoiMTgzdmoifQ.qBuoB08xDLxICpHHM2wUKohDJ_mXp5dWkWlKnPEbdeM>, 92% of Americans have noticed higher gas prices in their area, and 84% think prices will get even worse in the coming weeks. There is almost a direct relationship between high gas prices and lower presidential approval ratings. Gas prices tend to be even more politically damaging than higher prices for food, housing, or healthcare — for a couple of reasons. First, gas is a fairly inelastic part of the family budget. You can’t skimp on it. You still have to drive to work and get the kids to school, and there’s no generic alternative to shop around for. Second, the price is omnipresent — displayed on giant signs at every highway exit and street corner, going up a little more every time you drive by. Even so, voters generally don’t directly blame the President for a price increase. They usually blame oil companies, global affairs, or the general shittiness of greedy capitalism. Trump is in a uniquely bad position because voters specifically blame him. A Morning Consult <https://substack.com/redirect/1411f5f8-aeaf-4ab5-b3bb-f3768078edba?j=eyJ1IjoiMTgzdmoifQ.qBuoB08xDLxICpHHM2wUKohDJ_mXp5dWkWlKnPEbdeM> poll asked who or what was responsible: 48% said Trump, compared to 16% who pointed at oil and gas companies and 13% who blamed global market forces. What makes this even more politically painful is that gas prices were the one area where Trump could kinda, sorta show some progress — even if the decrease had nothing much to do with any of his actual policies. That’s bad. But it gets worse. Trump Never Prepared the Public In the run-up to the Iran War, Trump never tried to sell the country on the urgency of going to war. He didn’t go to Congress. He didn’t go to the United Nations. He didn’t give an address to the nation explaining his decision. He gave the longest State of the Union address in history and barely spent any time talking about Iran — ironically devoting nearly as much time to touting low gas prices as to making the case for war. War requires sacrifice. Wars in the Middle East almost always lead to higher gas prices. Trump never acknowledged that reality, let alone tried to explain why paying more at the pump — even temporarily — would be a worthwhile sacrifice. And because he didn’t, no one knows why we are at war with Iran. This is part of why the Iran War is currently the most unpopular foreign conflict in modern American history, per data compiled by the New York Times.<https://substack.com/redirect/bbe493e5-8546-4dce-876a-761b0706c4d2?j=eyJ1IjoiMTgzdmoifQ.qBuoB08xDLxICpHHM2wUKohDJ_mXp5dWkWlKnPEbdeM> So not only are people mad about high gas prices, they blame Trump, and they don’t like the war he started that raised them. I’m skeptical that Trump could have convinced people that war was a good idea and that higher gas prices were a worthwhile sacrifice. But the fact that he never even tried has made his political problems undoubtedly worse. The Political Consequences Cometh As everyone knows, Trump won the election by convincing Americans he would lower prices — not just defeat inflation, which is the rate at which prices rise, but actually bring down the cost of goods, housing, and healthcare. One half a semester of Econ 101 is enough to know that was never going to happen without plunging the economy into recession. Trump was going to pay a political price for that inevitable failure no matter what. But he has now taken two high-profile actions that actively raised prices. Liberation Day — when Trump announced his tariffs — was essentially the pivot point in his polling, the moment his approval numbers went definitively underwater. The Iran War is the second time Trump has looked voters in the eyes and said: not only am I not going to lower your costs, I’m going to raise them. Even if gas prices come down before November, Trump has destroyed the central premise of his entire presidency. His strength on the economy and prices was what held his coalition together. That’s gone now, and Republicans are facing a perfect storm in November, and Trump has no one to blame but himself. Share<https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=65026&post_id=191067357&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=false&r=183vj&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjoyMDU3ODg3LCJwb3N0X2lkIjoxOTEwNjczNTcsImlhdCI6MTc3Mzc0NDY3MSwiZXhwIjoxNzc2MzM2NjcxLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNjUwMjYiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.T4x7cmUT5bMXMRl7pw6w3oyxKlA9D_vLSCe_6_yfuM0> Thanks for subscribing to The Message Box<https://substack.com/redirect/e6db9350-c528-4157-ba05-77b8a59f6388?j=eyJ1IjoiMTgzdmoifQ.qBuoB08xDLxICpHHM2wUKohDJ_mXp5dWkWlKnPEbdeM>. Please help spread the word by sharing this post with your network. Thanks! Share<https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=65026&post_id=191067357&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=false&r=183vj&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjoyMDU3ODg3LCJwb3N0X2lkIjoxOTEwNjczNTcsImlhdCI6MTc3Mzc0NDY3MSwiZXhwIjoxNzc2MzM2NjcxLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNjUwMjYiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.T4x7cmUT5bMXMRl7pw6w3oyxKlA9D_vLSCe_6_yfuM0>Like<https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=65026&post_id=191067357&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=false&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjoyMDU3ODg3LCJwb3N0X2lkIjoxOTEwNjczNTcsInJlYWN0aW9uIjoi4p2kIiwiaWF0IjoxNzczNzQ0NjcxLCJleHAiOjE3NzYzMzY2NzEsImlzcyI6InB1Yi02NTAyNiIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.dXNHLBkml7MPFMldSFvoBZnm54LrJL6MIszFT-Oe4gw&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=183vj>Comment<https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=65026&post_id=191067357&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=false&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjoyMDU3ODg3LCJwb3N0X2lkIjoxOTEwNjczNTcsImlhdCI6MTc3Mzc0NDY3MSwiZXhwIjoxNzc2MzM2NjcxLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNjUwMjYiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.T4x7cmUT5bMXMRl7pw6w3oyxKlA9D_vLSCe_6_yfuM0&r=183vj&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email>Restack<https://substack..fzmni7nFecBdq58yjB4V04CPdo_KPCxBl5NWjWJX9ZQ?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email> © 2026 Dan Pfeiffer 548 Market Street PMB 72296, San Francisco, CA 94104 Unsubscribe<https://substack..31k8BsYJMz0gBer27BcnTdNlMbrHn2nrgHeckDFOLHc?> [Start writing]<https://substack..2uRJlpP5np7cx8ZLgpUB6rgeRU4Ew7wgfwN_oJpu4ek?>

item.title: Could Democrats Win Montana in 2026?
item.author: Dan Pfeiffer from The Message Box
item.content:encoded: Plus thoughts on Kristi Noem's fake job and Stephen Miller's toxicity ͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­ View in browser<https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubWVzc2FnZWJveG5ld3MuY29tL3AvY291bGQtZGVtb2NyYXRzLXdpbi1tb250YW5hLWluLTIwMjY_.13-uZeWPW44iwuNaZtWg6etsRfowWc0QnKsepvywTCw?><https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubWVzc2FnZWJveG5ld3MuY29tL3AvY291bGQtZGVtb2NyYXRzLXdpbi1tb250YW5hLWluLTIwMjY_.JfMoHze0Vk_X7GEdxpP0cYLMo1MEfMjdOXocY-VHVlg?> Could Democrats Win Montana in 2026?<https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=65026&post_id=190525351&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=false&r=183vj&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjoyMDU3ODg3LCJwb3N0X2lkIjoxOTA1MjUzNTEsImlhdCI6MTc3MzQ5MTQwNSwiZXhwIjoxNzc2MDgzNDA1LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNjUwMjYiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.j1hoVQaBdwlPkPQXzCbG-gJFo4wHFlpthwlHBPpIryA> Plus thoughts on Kristi Noem's fake job and Stephen Miller's toxicity Dan Pfeiffer<https://substack.com/@messagebox> Mar 14 ∙ Paid<https://substack.com/@messagebox><https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=65026&post_id=190525351&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=false&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjoyMDU3ODg3LCJwb3N0X2lkIjoxOTA1MjUzNTEsInJlYWN0aW9uIjoi4p2kIiwiaWF0IjoxNzczNDkxNDA1LCJleHAiOjE3NzYwODM0MDUsImlzcyI6InB1Yi02NTAyNiIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.q0OoeHynAJlaCGjyh-Xj2j5HmIxLPIVi0kImNWTcI6g&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=183vj><https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=65026&post_id=190525351&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=false&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjoyMDU3ODg3LCJwb3N0X2lkIjoxOTA1MjUzNTEsImlhdCI6MTc3MzQ5MTQwNSwiZXhwIjoxNzc2MDgzNDA1LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNjUwMjYiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.j1hoVQaBdwlPkPQXzCbG-gJFo4wHFlpthwlHBPpIryA&r=183vj&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email><https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=65026&post_id=190525351&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=false&r=183vj&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjoyMDU3ODg3LCJwb3N0X2lkIjoxOTA1MjUzNTEsImlhdCI6MTc3MzQ5MTQwNSwiZXhwIjoxNzc2MDgzNDA1LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNjUwMjYiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.j1hoVQaBdwlPkPQXzCbG-gJFo4wHFlpthwlHBPpIryA><https://substack..JBX65zJFkLQZjjkE4PXKSDI_D3hmao6DvRPqNr-S-Bc?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email> <https://open.substack.com/pub/messagebox/p/could-democrats-win-montana-in-2026?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app> READ IN APP Welcome to the mailbag! I really appreciate all the great questions and commentary that come with each of these posts. It’s one of the many things that make the Message Box community so special. A quick reminder: these mailbags run every Saturday as a special feature for paid subscribers. Subscribe to get full access and drop your questions for future mailbags.Subscribed<https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubWVzc2FnZWJveG5ld3MuY29tL2FjY291bnQiLCJwIjoxOTA1MjUzNTEsInMiOjY1MDI2LCJmIjpmYWxzZSwidSI6MjA1Nzg4NywiaWF0IjoxNzczNDkxNDA1LCJleHAiOjIwODkwNjc0MDUsImlzcyI6InB1Yi0wIiwic3ViIjoibGluay1yZWRpcmVjdCJ9.kuf0_CQlmEsGT-XdwVYjkjWEhmIkaBDpIMXFcxtbyWE?> Don’t forget to leave your questions for next week’s mailbag in the comments section. Leave a comment<https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=65026&post_id=190525351&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=false&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjoyMDU3ODg3LCJwb3N0X2lkIjoxOTA1MjUzNTEsImlhdCI6MTc3MzQ5MTQwNSwiZXhwIjoxNzc2MDgzNDA1LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNjUwMjYiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.j1hoVQaBdwlPkPQXzCbG-gJFo4wHFlpthwlHBPpIryA&r=183vj&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment> ________________________________ JF Hi Dan! Montana! How did Dems get flat-footed in this environment? Why isn’t Tester running like Sherrod Brown did? Answer Montana is a fascinating state that just got much more interesting. Recent events have made it a possible sleeper race to watch. It’s not so much that Democrats got caught flat-footed as it is that Republicans played incredibly dirty pool. Steve Daines was running for reelection until he wasn’t. About a minute before the filing deadline, Daines dropped out and U.S. Attorney Kurt Alme filed. This planned last-minute switcheroo prevented a GOP primary and kept any of Montana’s high-level Democrats — like Tester or former Governor Steve Bullock — from getting in the race. This is the same corrupt move that Democrat Chuy Garcia pulled in Chicago a few months ago. Daines was relatively popular, and I understand why Tester and Bullock might not have wanted to take him on. And to be clear, we don’t know if either of them wants to run for office again. Either way, something has to be done to stop incumbents from rigging elections by resigning at the last minute. One option: require incumbents to declare their intentions a few weeks before the filing deadline, giving others time to get in the race. Having said that, Montana is not off the table. It’s a long shot. Trump won Montana by 20 points in 2024, and Texas, Ohio, Iowa, Alaska, and even Florida are more likely wins for Democrats. And yet. If you squint, you can see a path. Trump’s tariffs and disastrous trade wars have had disproportionate effects on rural states that depend heavily on agriculture. Seth Bodnar, who just resigned as president of the University of Montana, is running as an independent with one of the best resumes I’ve ever seen — Green Beret, Rhodes Scholar, former GE executive. Former Democratic Senator Jon Tester has endorsed him despite the party’s desire to run their own candidate. Montana is one of those states where a candidate without the burden of the Democratic brand may have a better shot. But this only works if Democrats get behind Bodnar the way Nebraska Democrats did for Dan Osborn in 2024. Osborn lost, but came closer than anyone thought possible. Bodnar will have to prove his electability to clear the field, and if he does, Democrats will need to be willing to step aside for the greater good. With the war in Iran and surging gas prices making the Republicans’ already bad political situation worse, 2026 could be a perfect storm. That’s why I’ll be keeping my eye on Montana in the coming weeks. ________________________________ Katie Is there anyway to actually remove Stephen Miller from his position? He just feels like the puppeteer in so much of the worst of this administration. But he seems untouchable until he personally commits some Trump cardinal sin. Answer Unfortunately, not really. White House staff aren’t subject to Congressional approval — they serve at the pleasure of the president. The only person who can fire Miller is Donald Trump, which doesn’t seem particularly likely. That said, there’s value in making Miller as politically toxic as possible. If Republicans on the Hill start blaming him for their miserable political position, it could pressure Trump to fire him or at least reduce his power and influence within the administration. ________________________________ Norman Ramion Dan, with the November mid-terms quickly approaching, what are some key senate and house races where small dollar donations (like mine) can really help? In past messages on this topic, I believe you’ve said that direct contributions to individual campaigns can be most effective. However, are donations to the central Democratic organizations helpful at all, i.e., DCCC, DSCC, DNC? A refresher on how to make our modest donations have the most impact would be helpful. Answer I’m a firm believer that donating directly to campaigns is the most efficient use of money. Campaigns get lower rates on TV ads, can do direct electioneering, and cut out the middlemen. You also get to decide exactly where your money goes. If researching individual campaigns feels too daunting, Vote Save America has a fund that directs money to the races most important for flipping the House. Beyond winning the next election, we need to invest in long-term progressive infrastructure so we can compete in more places. Two good options: state parties, and Run for Something, an organization that recruits and trains candidates to run everywhere. I’ll try to put together a more exhaustive list at some point. ________________________________ Henry A. Wallace Super Fan Hey Dan, If you had to join one of the senate campaigns this cycle whose would it be and why? Answer I want my wife and two young children to know this is a thought exercise. I have no intention of joining a campaign. That said, this is a genuinely interesting question, so here are two answers. The first would be James Talarico’s campaign in Texas. Texas is the great white whale for Democrats — a fascinating state with complex demographics and politics. I think Talarico is a unique talent with a sophisticated understanding of modern politics and communications. It would be a hell of a race to be part of. The second is Jon Ossoff’s campaign in Georgia. Most people assume his reelection is a shoo-in. I don’t. He’s well-positioned, but Georgia is still a tough state, and there is no path to 51 Senate seats without re-electing Ossoff. The race is also critically important beyond the math — Ossoff is an intriguing 2028 dark horse, and a strong win would set him up as a young, exciting, electable candidate. There’s nothing more fun in politics than being on board when a rocketship takes off. ________________________________ Denise I understand that Noem’s new position is completely made up but I assume she will still be paid. Is there anything that can be done to keep her from pulling a paycheck? Answer While it hasn’t been officially confirmed, Noem will almost certainly get paid for this made-up job. The State Department creates “envoy” positions to handle specific problems or regions, and those positions are almost always paid. She’ll probably take a pay cut — the DHS Secretary makes $253,100 a year, and envoys typically make between $150,000 and $200,000. A future Democratic Congress could theoretically eliminate her position by prohibiting the use of funds for it in a State Department appropriations bill. If Trump were committed to paying her anyway, he could probably find another way to do it. ________________________________ Becka Phillipson Something interesting (to me) I saw in Times reporting of Texas Dem primary was that Crockett was +8 with Biden 2020 voters, while Talarico was +32 with Sanders voters (as of Wed morn). Some of this I suspect is correlations among demographics, but I’m curious whether that gives a hint about Talarico for the general: if he has broad appeal among Sanders voters, does that potentially translate to independents and Trump voters? (I’m thinking of the Mamdani-Trump voters, for example) Answer This caught my eye too, so I went looking for the same data and couldn’t find it. But those numbers make sense, and I think they can be explained this way. According to exit polls from the 2020 Texas primary, Biden got 58% of Black voters while Sanders got only 15%. Sanders beat Biden by 13 points with Latino voters, and they essentially tied among white voters. While we don’t have exit polls from the 2026 Texas primary, pre-election polling and early precinct data suggest Talarico ran well with white and Latino voters while Crockett built huge margins elsewhere. Like Mamdani, I think Talarico has real appeal to independents and disaffected Republicans. That said, Texas and New York City could not be more different politically, and the formula for building that coalition would almost certainly look different in practice. ________________________________ Nicole On PSA after the SOTU, you told Favs you didn’t think most Americans are stupid enough to fall for the Repubs’ ad about Dems being crazy for not standing when Trump said the most important role of the government is protecting citizens from immigrants. I’ve seen lots of compelling evidence to the contrary; lots of Americans seem pretty dumb to me. Do you really think most Americans are “smart” or at least savvy enough to not fall for that kind of propaganda. Answer I get the skepticism. The idea that American voters are smart — after Trump won two of the last four elections — is a tough sell. But I largely stand by it. Maybe the better way to put it is this: most voters are rational in their self-interest. The swing voters who went for Trump in 2024 made a misguided decision, but not an irrational one. This is an oversimplification, but the logic went something like: they were getting crushed by inflation. Even if they didn’t fully blame Biden for the price increases, they felt he didn’t do enough to address them. His Vice President didn’t make a compelling case about what she’d do differently. So they voted for the guy who was president the last time they felt good about the economy. That hasn’t worked out well — for them or for us — but you can follow the logic. Beyond that, I think most voters outside the hardcore partisan base have a pretty good BS detector. They’re deeply skeptical of politicians and political attacks in general. That’s primarily why I don’t think this particular, unsubtle attack will land. ________________________________ Okay, that’s it for this week. Leave your questions for next week in the comments. Leave a comment<https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=65026&post_id=190525351&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=false&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjoyMDU3ODg3LCJwb3N0X2lkIjoxOTA1MjUzNTEsImlhdCI6MTc3MzQ5MTQwNSwiZXhwIjoxNzc2MDgzNDA1LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNjUwMjYiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.j1hoVQaBdwlPkPQXzCbG-gJFo4wHFlpthwlHBPpIryA&r=183vj&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment> Thanks for subscribing to The Message Box<https://substack.com/redirect/4edce5c3-ee3f-4fc7-bab4-1d9609274e0f?j=eyJ1IjoiMTgzdmoifQ.qBuoB08xDLxICpHHM2wUKohDJ_mXp5dWkWlKnPEbdeM>. Please help spread the word by sharing this post with your network. Thanks! Share<https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=65026&post_id=190525351&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=false&r=183vj&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjoyMDU3ODg3LCJwb3N0X2lkIjoxOTA1MjUzNTEsImlhdCI6MTc3MzQ5MTQwNSwiZXhwIjoxNzc2MDgzNDA1LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNjUwMjYiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.j1hoVQaBdwlPkPQXzCbG-gJFo4wHFlpthwlHBPpIryA>Like<https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=65026&post_id=190525351&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=false&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjoyMDU3ODg3LCJwb3N0X2lkIjoxOTA1MjUzNTEsInJlYWN0aW9uIjoi4p2kIiwiaWF0IjoxNzczNDkxNDA1LCJleHAiOjE3NzYwODM0MDUsImlzcyI6InB1Yi02NTAyNiIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.q0OoeHynAJlaCGjyh-Xj2j5HmIxLPIVi0kImNWTcI6g&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=183vj>Comment<https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=65026&post_id=190525351&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=false&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjoyMDU3ODg3LCJwb3N0X2lkIjoxOTA1MjUzNTEsImlhdCI6MTc3MzQ5MTQwNSwiZXhwIjoxNzc2MDgzNDA1LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNjUwMjYiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.j1hoVQaBdwlPkPQXzCbG-gJFo4wHFlpthwlHBPpIryA&r=183vj&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email>Restack<https://substack..JBX65zJFkLQZjjkE4PXKSDI_D3hmao6DvRPqNr-S-Bc?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email> © 2026 Dan Pfeiffer 548 Market Street PMB 72296, San Francisco, CA 94104 Unsubscribe<https://substack..QKgWda5Po3fhu4pFQosSPpa6MCB-TBI6wt7pP3l22ps?> [Start writing]<https://substack..CWTKYMPmLyWg-0RZuR2JyWFAnrWLzeD2PdagtePCHL8?>

item.title: No Democrat Should Give Trump a Blank Check for His Illegal War
item.author: Dan Pfeiffer from The Message Box
item.content:encoded: The case for opposing funding for Trump's War in Iran ͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­͏   ­ Forwarded this email? Subscribe here<https://substack..UoPSVuHQCY0xoEKSsyUV85mOt8fd-bLDZ66Qfa0SoOg?> for more<https://substack..ek8ZMUZy6GUep8J0XYbv14M-Kn22PqRcB8RFyZyk_zw?> No Democrat Should Give Trump a Blank Check for His Illegal War<https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=65026&post_id=190410323&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=false&r=183vj&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjoyMDU3ODg3LCJwb3N0X2lkIjoxOTA0MTAzMjMsImlhdCI6MTc3MzIyNTA3MywiZXhwIjoxNzc1ODE3MDczLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNjUwMjYiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.iXg8jjfUgXDv33iVQ4zl3RDWkvd1Kv2QMqS7crg6mLw> The case for opposing funding for Trump's War in Iran Dan Pfeiffer<https://substack.com/@messagebox> Mar 11<https://substack.com/@messagebox><https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=65026&post_id=190410323&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=false&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjoyMDU3ODg3LCJwb3N0X2lkIjoxOTA0MTAzMjMsInJlYWN0aW9uIjoi4p2kIiwiaWF0IjoxNzczMjI1MDczLCJleHAiOjE3NzU4MTcwNzMsImlzcyI6InB1Yi02NTAyNiIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.6YCvmiOTxvkRKqtsAca3HIJLV3pBGnYyuduX7gw4Lpk&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=183vj><https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=65026&post_id=190410323&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=false&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjoyMDU3ODg3LCJwb3N0X2lkIjoxOTA0MTAzMjMsImlhdCI6MTc3MzIyNTA3MywiZXhwIjoxNzc1ODE3MDczLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNjUwMjYiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.iXg8jjfUgXDv33iVQ4zl3RDWkvd1Kv2QMqS7crg6mLw&r=183vj&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email><https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=65026&post_id=190410323&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=false&r=183vj&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjoyMDU3ODg3LCJwb3N0X2lkIjoxOTA0MTAzMjMsImlhdCI6MTc3MzIyNTA3MywiZXhwIjoxNzc1ODE3MDczLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNjUwMjYiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.iXg8jjfUgXDv33iVQ4zl3RDWkvd1Kv2QMqS7crg6mLw><https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9vcGVuLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9wdWIvbWVzc2FnZWJveC9wL25vLWRlbW9jcmF0LXNob3VsZC1naXZlLXRydW1wLWEtYmxhbms_.DF2dMQf-dhwBDkeuBB3d7AaeCYW4tjeoyOfY0XiZn_c?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email> <https://open.substack.com/pub/messagebox/p/no-democrat-should-give-trump-a-blank?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app> READ IN APP Trump’s illegal regime change war in Iran has reached its second week, and things are not going well. Yes, the U.S. and Israel are bombing the hell out of Iran, but the region has been thrust into complete chaos. Iran just appointed a new Ayatollah who is vehemently anti-American, with no signs of the complete surrender that Trump has demanded. The war is costing the U.S. at least a billion dollars a day. The Strait of Hormuz is closed, and gas prices have This was a strategic and moral disaster. Democrats have almost universally opposed the war. Most have aggressively criticized Trump for his capricious decision to go to war. And yet — there is always an And Yet — many Democrats who oppose the war seem prepared to turn around and vote to fund it. A number of Democrats have refused to rule out voting for the $50 billion that Trump is going to request to pay for the war. This would be a catastrophic mistake.Subscribed<https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubWVzc2FnZWJveG5ld3MuY29tL2FjY291bnQiLCJwIjoxOTA0MTAzMjMsInMiOjY1MDI2LCJmIjpmYWxzZSwidSI6MjA1Nzg4NywiaWF0IjoxNzczMjI1MDczLCJleHAiOjIwODg4MDEwNzMsImlzcyI6InB1Yi0wIiwic3ViIjoibGluay1yZWRpcmVjdCJ9._bRDallps2VnHBLGGAOtPO90vcRuaPHevWrUipIHeu0?> ________________________________ The Moral Case In the run-up to and aftermath of the Iran War, Trump and his administration have been unable to answer basic questions about why we are at war, what the plan is, or what victory looks like. Is the goal to prevent Iran from getting a nuclear weapon? If so, why did Trump say he’d already obliterated their program just two weeks ago? If the goal is regime change, what kind of regime do we want? Trump has said he wants freedom for the Iranian people — and also that he’d be comfortable with a Venezuela-style situation where the current regime stays in place. He has also called for complete and total surrender. And if the goal is to inspire a pro-American uprising, why are we bombing civilian infrastructure? The Trump administration can’t make a coherent case for this war because there isn’t one. They made this momentous decision with almost no thought to its potentially calamitous consequences. Eight American soldiers are dead. 150 more have been wounded. More than a hundred Iranian schoolgirls and many more civilians have been killed. Struggling American families are paying more at the pump. And for what? No articulated strategic imperative. No imminent threat. Trump started a war just because he thought he could — without asking Congress for authorization or building any public consent. It’s one of the most irresponsible decisions made by a U.S. president in modern history. Democrats are right to oppose it, and they would be wrong to fund it. Many have drawn comparisons to the Iraq War, when Democrats repeatedly voted to pay for a conflict many of them opposed. Even then-Senator Obama voted for Iraq funding bills. That muscle memory is causing so many Democrats to be open to funding Trump’s war today. Maybe those votes were a mistake — I’m open to that critique. But this is a fundamentally different situation. Congress authorized the Iraq War. Bush went through the steps he was supposed to take. And there were troops on the ground that needed armored Humvees and other supplies. None of that is true here. This war is illegal. Trump never sought authorization. The war is on such shaky legal footing that some Republicans won’t even use the word “war” to describe it. The whole thing is enough to make Kafka blush. Because Trump and the Republicans won’t seek authorization, the only lever Democrats have to stop this war is the funding. That is not a power they should cede lightly. Trump is reportedly considering deploying ground troops. That can only happen if Democrats fund the war. Voting to fund it is handing Donald Trump a blank check. Any Democrat who does so will bear responsibility for whatever comes next — in a war being waged by an irrational and unpredictable president with no plan and no endgame. ________________________________ The Political Case I know it can feel trite to talk about politics in moments like these, but we are in a high-stakes fight for the future of this country. The only way to stop Trump and protect democracy is to win more political power. In that sense, thinking about politics isn’t a distraction — it’s a moral obligation. And the political case against funding this war could not be more compelling. First, nobody wants this war. A Quinnipiac poll released Monday found 53% oppose and only 40% support — a number consistent across all the polling since the war began. That includes a significant chunk of Trump voters. Approval for the Iran War basically tracks Trump’s overall approval rating, which tells you something important: almost no one who still approves of Trump is a persuadable voter in 2026. We are trying to win over the majority of Americans who disapprove of Trump and his war. Second, even the war’s supporters aren’t really into it. In most polls, the number of people who strongly disapprove of this war is roughly twice as large as the number who strongly approve. Third, the American people don’t see Iran as a serious threat. An NPR/Marist poll found that 55% of Americans — including 60% of independents — see Iran as a minor threat or no threat at all. Finally, voters don’t trust Trump to wage war. There are some Democrats genuinely wrestling with whether they have an obligation to fund troops in the field. But most are simply afraid of being painted as anti-military by Trump, Fox News, and the right-wing media machine. These Democrats were around — or came of age — in post-9/11 America. They remember Max Cleland being compared to Osama Bin Laden. Here’s the thing: those attacks only work when the person making them has credibility on national security. Trump doesn’t. In the Quinnipiac poll, 55% disapprove of his performance as Commander in Chief, 57% disapprove of him on foreign policy, and 57% disapprove of his handling of Iran. These are brutal numbers. The polling makes the case, but there’s a bigger strategic imperative here too. To vote to fund is to give Trump a veneer of bipartisan cover for an illegal, costly war that nobody wants. Why would Democrats do that? Why would they accept shared responsibility for a war they believe is a bad idea, poorly executed, and actively damaging the economy? The internal Democratic polling I’ve seen shows that the most powerful argument against this war connects the billions being spent in Iran to what’s being cut at home — a trillion dollars slashed from Medicaid, rural hospitals closing, Americans losing their health coverage. Voting to fund the war hands Trump a free pass on all of it. And there’s something even bigger at stake. Voters already think Democrats are too weak to stand up to Trump. Some of that criticism is fair. Some of it reflects unrealistic expectations. But voting to fund this war — at this moment — would validate that criticism in the most vivid and politically catastrophic way imaginable. It's not enough for Democratic leaders to sit back and see where the wind blows. This is exactly the moment voters are watching to see whether Democrats actually stand for something. Fight to stop this funding, and bring as many Democrats with them as possible.Subscribed<https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubWVzc2FnZWJveG5ld3MuY29tL2FjY291bnQiLCJwIjoxOTA0MTAzMjMsInMiOjY1MDI2LCJmIjpmYWxzZSwidSI6MjA1Nzg4NywiaWF0IjoxNzczMjI1MDczLCJleHAiOjIwODg4MDEwNzMsImlzcyI6InB1Yi0wIiwic3ViIjoibGluay1yZWRpcmVjdCJ9._bRDallps2VnHBLGGAOtPO90vcRuaPHevWrUipIHeu0?> Share<https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=65026&post_id=190410323&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=false&r=183vj&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjoyMDU3ODg3LCJwb3N0X2lkIjoxOTA0MTAzMjMsImlhdCI6MTc3MzIyNTA3MywiZXhwIjoxNzc1ODE3MDczLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNjUwMjYiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.iXg8jjfUgXDv33iVQ4zl3RDWkvd1Kv2QMqS7crg6mLw> Thanks for subscribing to The Message Box<https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubWVzc2FnZWJveG5ld3MuY29tP3V0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj1lbWFpbC1ob21lJnI9MTgzdmoiLCJwIjoxOTA0MTAzMjMsInMiOjY1MDI2LCJmIjpmYWxzZSwidSI6MjA1Nzg4NywiaWF0IjoxNzczMjI1MDczLCJleHAiOjIwODg4MDEwNzMsImlzcyI6InB1Yi0wIiwic3ViIjoibGluay1yZWRpcmVjdCJ9.QSE8MjBFKW2OH7jQ1TMvMKEumf_RcsCkiK3-4_gSt-I?>. Please help spread the word by sharing this post.
